content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 27, 1990


Dana Appling
Sacramento Municipal Utility District
P.O. Box 15830
Sacramento, CA  95852-1830

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-058

Dear Ms. Appling:

You have requested advice regarding the interpretation of
Section 87200 of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Because
your inquiry is of a general nature and does not refer to the
reporting requirements of specific individuals, we treat your
request for advice as a request for informal assistance.¹ This
letter confirms telephone advice provided February 22, 1990.

QUESTION

Are the filing requirements imposed upon "other public of-
ficials who manage public investments" under Section 87200 limited
to persons who manage city, county, or state funds?

CONCLUSION

The term "other public officials who manage public invest-
ments" is not limited to persons who manage the investment of
city, county, or state funds. It includes those who any
public funds, including the investment of funds of a municipal
utility district.

_______________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Our File No. I-90-058
Page 2


FACTS

You are an attorney for the Sacramento Municipal Utility
District (the "District"), a public agency. District revenues
from rates charged customers and the return on investment of the
surplus revenues are public funds, but they are not city, county,
or state funds. You have requested advice as to whether recent
amendments to Section 87200 of the Act apply to persons who manage
the District's investments in light of advice contained in our
1989/90 manual for Statement of Economic Interests.

ANALYSIS

Section 87200 of the Act was amended effective January 1,
1990, to include additional persons who must file Form 721, statement of Economic Interests, with the Commission. Persons who are
included in Section 87200 are subject to broad conflict-of-
interest disclosure requirements. As a result of the recent
amendment, Section 87200 now applies to city and county treasurers
and "other public officials who manage public investments."

The attached letter to interested persons dated February 27,
1990, sets forth in general terms the types of persons who are
included within the term "other public officials who manage public
investments." As indicated, the term covers persons who manage
the investments of all public agencies and is not limited to those
persons who manage city, county or state funds.

Your letter refers to a provision in our 1989/90 Statement of
Economic Interest manual setting forth some interim guidelines for
interpretation of the persons covered. That manual provides that
Section 87200 now applies to:

All other public officials who manage public
investments, including but not limited to:

Employees and consultants who have decision
making authority with regard to investment of city,
county or state funds. This includes, but is not
limited to, pension fund managers for public
agencies....

(1989/90 manual Form 730,
emphasis added.)

As the above excerpt indicates, the term "public investments" is
not limited to the investment of city, county, or state funds.

We are currently in the process of developing regulations to
implement and clarify the scope of the term "other public of-
ficials who manage public investments." Please contact us if you
have any questions with respect to the application of the term to
specific individuals.

Our File No. I-90-058
Page 3


The Act does not contain a provision permitting us to waive
or extend filing dates for specific individuals. As I indicated
to you in our conversation of February 22, individuals coming
within the category of public officials who manage public
investments, who have been filing Form 731, may continue to do so
for the 1989 reporting period. If you have any first-time filers
who qualify under Section 87200, they must file Form 721 for the
1989 reporting period. The filing dates are set forth in footnote
2 of the attached letter.

I trust that this answers your questions regarding this is-
sue. If you require any additional information, you may contact
me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Margaret W. Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:MWE:aa

Attachment
